risk_flags: Strait of Hormuz closure risk premium embedded in forward curves; NATO de-escalation signal could trigger 5–10% TTF downside if LNG arb unwinds.
Alpine hydro vulnerability if May heatwave persists into June; intraday balancing cost and DA spread volatility will spike.
Clean Spark at 86th pctile (26.88 EUR/MWh) signals gas is deeply in-the-money vs coal; Hormuz escalation would extend spark margin further.
EU growth forecast cut explicitly pins energy inflation as driver; sustained power curve elevation depends on geopolitical risk premium persisting.

base.summary: Quiet fundamentals; Hormuz premium contained; summer heatwave elevates cooling demand but renewables at 73rd pctile provide offset.

downside.summary: NATO intervention de-escalates Hormuz closure risk; LNG arb unwinds; heatwave eases by early June.

upside.summary: Hormuz escalation deepens; LNG arb tightens; heatwave hydro stress in Alps; EU policy tightening accelerates.

themes: Hormuz disruption risk embedded in LNG-TTF arb; NATO intervention signal offers downside
Record May heatwave stress-testing Alpine hydro and summer cooling demand
EU growth cut cites energy inflation; medium-term policy tightening on cards
US industrial gas demand surge reducing LNG export availability for NW Europe
top_drivers: Hormuz disruption risk premium embedded in LNG and TTF; NATO intervention narrative offers 5–10% downside relief if de-escalation sentiment firms.
May heatwave front-loading summer cooling demand and stressing Alpine hydro; intraday balancing and DA-ID spreads to remain elevated through June.
EU growth cut explicitly links energy inflation to economic slowdown; sustained policy debate signals potential medium-term power curve support via supply tightening.
top_takeaway: Quiet fundamentals mask geopolitical Hormuz tail-risk; May heatwave stress-tests summer demand, but NATO intervention signal could deflate LNG premium.

watchlist.metric: Strait of Hormuz disruption risk premium
watchlist.why: NATO intervention narrative could deflate embedded LNG spot/forward premium and weaken TTF by 5–10% if closure risk sentiment shifts.
Alpine/Central European hydro depletion and peak-summer cooling demand front-loading will elevate intraday volatility and DA-ID spreads through June.
Storage mandates, LNG contracts, and interconnect investments will reshape fuel-mix economics and medium-term power curve risk allocation.

watchlist.metric: US industrial nat-gas demand (2026–2027 record forecast)
watchlist.why: Peak US industrial consumption shrinks LNG export availability for NW Europe, supporting TTF winter curve via tighter spot import arb.

watchlist_dated.name: NATO security meeting — Hormuz intervention signal
watchlist_dated.why: Outcome could trigger sharp unwind of LNG closure premium, weakening TTF spot and Cal+1 by 5–8%.
